slides.title: Building on Existing Code 🧬
slides.content:
# What is Inheritance?

**Inheritance** lets a class reuse code from another:

```python
class Animal:      # Parent/Base class
    pass

class Dog(Animal): # Child class inherits
    pass
```

Dog gets everything Animal has!

slides.title: Basic Inheritance 💻

quizData.question: What does inheritance provide?
quizData.explanation: Inheritance lets child classes reuse code from parent classes!

slides.title: Method Overriding 🔄
slides.content:
# Customizing Inherited Methods

```python
class Cat(Animal):
    def speak(self):  # Override parent
        print(f"{self.name} says: Meow!")
```

Child classes can override parent methods!

slides.title: Override Example 💻

quizData.question: What is method overriding?

options.text: Redefining a parent method in child

quizData.explanation: Overriding means the child class provides its own version of a parent method!

slides.title: Using super() 🦸
slides.content:
# Calling Parent Methods

```python
class Dog(Animal):
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        super().__init__(name)  # Call parent
        self.breed = breed
```

`super()` accesses the parent class!

slides.title: super() Example 💻
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Is-A Relationship

Use inheritance when there's an "is-a" relationship:

* ✅ Dog **is an** Animal
* ✅ Car **is a** Vehicle
* ❌ Engine **is a** Car (has-a instead!)

quizData.question: What does super() do?
quizData.explanation: super() lets you call methods from the parent class!
